operating_point: rich BHI, T=40 C, v2 posterior-median tuned params
what_binds: two REDUNDANT enzyme-mass (proteome) pools bind in sequence: the etcgem sMOMENT sector pool at ~1.44 (relaxing it -> 1.55), then the GECKO base total-protein pool prot_pool_exchange (0.091 g/gDW) at ~1.55 (relaxing all proteome -> 7.6). Named binding constraint: the enzyme-mass pool (NOT O2, glucose uptake, ATPM, GAM, or a biomass precursor -- all had slack).
why_kcat_saturates: kcat_scale scales only the etcgem per-flux cost, so it relaxes the etcgem pool but NOT the GECKO base pool; kcat therefore saturates at the GECKO-pool ceiling ~1.55.
classification: PROTEOME (enzyme-mass) ceiling, NOT non-proteome/structural. Partly a FIXABLE inconsistency (the GECKO base pool 0.091 is tighter than and unreconciled with the emergent P_total x sigma budget).

relax_culprit_scan_both_pools.note: scale BOTH enzyme-mass pools x mult -> rmax

interpretation: Reaching the observed ~2.4 needs ~1.8x more total enzyme budget (~sigma 0.45 -> ~0.77, above the 0.4-0.5 literature range) OR reconciling the redundant GECKO base pool. So the magnitude gap IS proteome and closable in principle; the last ~1.7x may reflect a genuinely higher in-vivo enzyme capacity on rich medium (sigma / P_total) rather than a network/structural gap.
